programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. programme.desc: A newly engaged couple are joined by Jules Hudson on a house-hunting mission in south Worcestershire, looking to purchase their first joint home with a budget of £300,000(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Robin goes head to head with 81-year-old breeder Di in the National Competition at the Royal Norfolk Show, showcasing their diminutive Dexter cattle(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Christina Trevanion's trip reaches its third leg in Somerset and Devon, where Tim examines a Boer War pipe and Christina finds a Victorian lectern(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Labour Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern help members of the Humberside Police force combat their sedentary behaviour as they challenge them to bring more movement into their daily routines, fitting them with movement trackers to check their progress. Meanwhile, Hugh tests one of the most popular health crazes to hit the market, CBD, on three volunteers who suffer from insomnia, and Steph meets a family struggling with issues surrounding their body image(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Goffy wants Danielle to know he is sorry for leading a double life. Meanwhile, Aoife has returned from Australia and Conner is keen to start things up again(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Cardiff-based 20-year-old hopeless romantic Lewis is scaring away potential partners by talking wedding plans on the first date(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators look into an IT support worker shamelessly helping himself to hospital computers and a gang illegally fly-tipping on an industrial scale(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Carl and Danielle want to move to Australia but a health scare has shown them how fickle life can be. A week in Perth helps them decide whether they could leave their family behind(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and finds out how the village of Portmeirion became the birthplace of some famous pottery(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A visit from an old friend forces Karen to confront her past. A toy gun battle for Joe's birthday becomes a little too real and Bear tries to get in on Ayesha and Valerie's party(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Food Festival plays host to first-generation farmer Sam, with his rare breed of pork, and the family run Kerry Vale Vineyard, selling their English wine(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The search reaches the New Forest, and after an encounter with some ponies, Tim Medhurst buys a horse, while Christina Trevanion turns farrier. Other items include a George III commode and some Scottish treasure(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Green Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Matt Baker and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick regrets admitting to Linda that Ollie might be better off without her, but before he can apologise his wife storms out. Whitney goes to the Slaters' to apologise to Kat, giving Leo the chance he needs to search for the letter. A night out for the Albert Square youngsters start badly as Dennis is less than pleased to see Bobby - and things quickly go downhill from there(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A desert island survival course goes wrong when the leader - a trained survival expert and ex-SAS soldier - is found dead in the forest of a remote island near Saint Marie. Having ventured off into the wilderness alone and with no other people on the island apart from the trip participants, suspicion soon falls upon them. But with all of the course members providing one another with an alibi, how could any of them have committed the murder? That's what new detective inspector Neville intends to find out. Starring Ralf Little(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate in Dundee, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: The deal may have been signed and the name may have changed but it's business as usual for the Brexitcast team as Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming deliver their usual mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest goings-on at Westminster(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Hornetts sample a week in Brisbane and while Simon has dreamt of making it his home, his wife Joanna has strong emotional ties to the UK that she would find very hard to break(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for bargains at Wetherby Racecourse, while Charlie Ross learns the history of an unusual chapel in Knaresborough that is hundreds of years old and carved into rock(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Hull FC v Hull Kingston Rovers at KCOM Stadium, Warrington v St Helens at Halliwell Jones Stadium and Castleford Tigers v Wigan Warriors at Mend-a-Hose Jungle(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team search for ideal locations for seaside leisure, where they can enjoy their personal passions by the coast(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Alex Jones talks to Brian Conley about her small-screen favourites, while reminiscing about her childhood. The One Show presenter re-enacts scenes from her favourite kids' show, reveals which popular comedian terrified her as a youngster and how a mix-up led to her big break. She also discusses her family's love of hidden-camera shows and how the family cat made the telly explode(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Keith needs an alibi for the arson, but hates his dad's solution. Sharon picks up a small-time dealer, who Laser later targets(n)

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: The documentary offering a glimpse into the rarely seen South American wilderness continues, this time following the path of the relentless wind sweeping east through Patagonia's dry desert from the Andes' peaks. The programme reveals a weird world of maras - a large relative of the guinea pig - and penguins, and witnesses the first faltering steps of baby guanacos - Patagonia's very own camels. Cameras also observe the people who have taken on this inhospitable environment and carved out a home for themselves. Santiago Cabrera narrates(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's forests, and see how the pine marten in Scotland is helping animals in Wales(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot discovers a valuable sculpture at Rochester Cathedral in Kent and Raj Bisram finds a ghostly Victorian photo album(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the largest island of the Indonesian archipelago, travelling through rainforests and paddy fields from the capital, Jakarta, to the volcanic region at the heart of Java's Central Province. He visits Java's most dangerous volcano, Mount Merapi, learns how to make noodles, and visits the Borobudur Temple, a Buddhist shrine that lay buried under a layer of volcanic ash for centuries(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Plus, back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The largest boats in the fishing fleet head out to sea in autumn, including giant netters and trawlers up to 100 feet long capable of fishing hundreds of miles from shore. On one boat, an engineer and deckhand with 10 years of service behind him is training to become a captain, under the watchful eye of his veteran skipper. The vessel faces a rough week at sea in gale force winds. Back on land, the owner of a shellfish company that employs workers from Latvia and Lithuania fears for the future as Britain leaves the EU(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Liverpool's busiest jobcentre in Toxteth. Among new applicants for Universal Credit is a woman made redundant at 62, who is under huge pressure to find work. A long-standing veteran of jobcentres reveals how the new benefits system is struggling to accommodate everyone, and a pregnant woman who came to Liverpool from the Czech Republic as a child is made to take a test to prove that she is a long-term resident in the city. Elsewhere, cameras follow the senior civil servant responsible for Jobcentres as he struggles to cope with moving 100,000 people onto universal credit in the wake of a shrinking budget(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has her first session with her therapist, and heeds his advice on the need for closure with her relationship with Xander. Guest starring Matthew Broderick(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: The Flu That Killed 50 Million
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the 1918 Flu pandemic, which killed more than 50 million people. Using dramatic reconstruction and eyewitness testimony from doctors, soldiers, civilians and politicians, this one-off brings to life the onslaught of the disease and the horrors of those who lived through it. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Six couples are challenged to prove their survival skills in Alaska's harsh wilderness, with the most successful pair winning a remarkable home miles from the nearest road, which was built from scratch by its original owners. In the first episode, they receive a crash course in what to do when encountering a bear before being sent into the woods to gather material and build shelters. They must then fell trees, make a fire and brave the freezing waters of Lost Lake(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine the violent assault and murder of a teenage girl in 1918, and how a button and badge found near her body led to the arrest of an ex-serviceman(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A specialist surgeon travels to Shetland to carry out a complex operation, while a Fair Isle man hopes to get the all-clear after surgery to find out if cancer has been kept at bay(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mum Rachael thinks she and her son Harry should move to Australia for a better quality of life. Harry, however, is not so sure, so the pair spend a trial week in Brisbane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill explores the county of Cornwall to find out how the dramatic landscape shapes the lives and beliefs of the people who live there(n)

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part series in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Claire learns some home truths, and Sharon works on Robbie to turn informer, although he objects. While new to the station, it appears Ash Cahill is no stranger locally(n)

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Life in the treacherous seas that surround the South American wilderness. The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke and Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the waters surrounding the UK, discovering how the white-tailed sea eagle has made a dramatic return to the coast(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, with brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers the fascinating stories of the people of Berwick-upon-Tweed's Paxton House, past and present.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to Java's Central Province and the royal city of Yogyakarta, where he visits the Sultan's Palace and admires the ancient art of shadow puppetry. Moving north to Ambawara, Michael boards a scenic heritage line constructed by the Dutch with carriages dating from the time of his Bradshaw's guidebook(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether yoga can lower your blood pressure as much as a workout, and Alain Gregoire tests meal replacement shakes to see if they are as healthy as they claim. Meanwhile, Zoe Williams discovers why wearing your contact lenses in the shower damage your eyesight, and Giles Yeo tests whether gourmet salts are any healthier than table salt(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Aziraphale and Crowley race towards Tadfield airbase as they attempt to prevent Adam and the Four Horsemen from beginning the apocalypse, but one has been discorporated and the other is trapped on a flaming motorway. Will they get there in time? Starring Michael Sheen, David Tennant, Adria Arjona and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Romesh: Talking to Comedians
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hangs out in a pie-and-mash shop with comedy actress Sally Phillips, talking about being parents to special children, inappropriate unicorn costume days and drenching school teachers(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Henning Wehn hosts from London's Hammersmith Apollo, welcoming Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Liverpool's busiest jobcentre in Toxteth. Among new applicants for Universal Credit is a woman made redundant at 62, who is under huge pressure to find work. A long-standing veteran of jobcentres reveals how the new benefits system is struggling to accommodate everyone, and a pregnant woman who came to Liverpool from the Czech Republic as a child is made to take a test to prove that she is a long-term resident in the city. Elsewhere, cameras follow the senior civil servant responsible for Jobcentres as he struggles to cope with moving 100,000 people onto universal credit in the wake of a shrinking budget(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP continues his journey across Europe in Poland and Germany. In Leczna, eastern Poland, he meets coal miners who explain the importance of an industry that is now under threat from the EU. Crossing the border to Germany, Ed heads to Dorfchemnitz, which in the 2017 national election returned the highest vote in the country for the far-right Alternative for Germany party. He also visits Bremen, a city that has felt the impact of Angela Merkel's controversial policy to take on more refugees during the migrant crisis than any other country in Europe(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows the Edwards family during a trial week in Perth as mum Shana tries to persuade reluctant husband Philip and their two older children, who are adamant about not going, that Australia is the life for them(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, Caroline Hawley uncovers a remarkable collection of suffragette postcards and David Harper is delighted by some Hull Daily Mail newspaper printing blocks, saved from the tip. Presented by Paul Martin(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Westerglen Transmitting Station in Falkirk, before visiting Gleneagles and Berry Town on route to his final stop on this leg, the city of Dundee(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: It's the first full week of the challenge and Tom is back in Gloucester, the city where he grew up, to exercise and cook alongside his diet and fitness gang. As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig follows in the footsteps of Queen Victoria in the Cairngorms National Park, visiting a church that was built for the monarch but not completed until after her death(n)

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: With Sharon out of action, Robbie's fate is sealed. Niamh's bad behaviour results in betrayal and the Hennessys are desperate to protect themselves(n)

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan narrates a documentary following capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis as they strive to raise their families. The first episode sees the young animals taking their first steps in some of the world's most extraordinary habitats, where they have to beware of deadly predators including the jaguar and caiman(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: The wildlife of the mountains, finding out about ravens, the golden eagles of the Scottish hills and asking whether the UK will ever again play host to a long-lost beast - the lynx(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Natasha Raskin Sharp are at Bristol Cathedral to assess a range of items, while presenter Paul Martin discovers the darker side of the city's trading history(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through south-east Asia, learning how coffee was first brought to Java for cultivation, visiting the headquarters of the Dutch East Indies Railway Company, and ending at Surabaya, a port bursting with local traditions, produce and culture(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Charlotte Smith and Tom Heap swap agriculture for antiques as they search Hampshire and Buckinghamshire for bargain collectibles, assisted by auctioneers James Braxton and Phil Serrell. Their final destination is an auction in Winchester - but before that, Tom turns woodworker as he learns the ancient craft of 'bodging' in the Chiltern hills, while Charlotte meets the famous Aylesbury duck - common to the area for 300 years and the inspiration behind Jemima Puddle-Duck(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Project manager Alice and her team unframe and scan a gallery of 500-year-old masterpieces - Raphael's cartoons, removing frames that have sealed them in for 30 years. While in the V&A's stunning jewellery galleries, newly-appointed keeper James must check some of the most fragile objects in the collection - the jewellery made with moving parts. Among the treasures are a spectacular butterfly ring worn by Beyoncé that appears to take flight with each gentle flex of the finger(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Return of the documentary showing the challenges faced by the NHS, focusing on the work of seven trusts across Liverpool, whose hospitals stretch to north Wales, Cheshire and the Isle of Man. The city's new state-of-the-art hospital remains unfinished, three years after it was due to open, so two of the biggest hospital trusts are planning an ambitious merger. But this means that hundreds of patients and staff have to be transported across the city, while all the operating theatres bar one are closed(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: The family are stuck indoors as the weather takes a turn for the worse. Pauline receives a visitor and Cathy comes to an important decision. Comedy, starring Lesley Manville(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: As winter draws in and the weather grows wilder, the remaining couples are challenged to clear a path through a dense area of the wilderness for a dog sled team in just three hours. Once the work is completed, the captain of the sled team selects one of the couples to visit Ose Mountain, where they must clear debris from a reservoir and learn how to correctly butcher an animal in the wild(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford examines the story of Andreas Vesalius, who in 1537 became the most famous anatomist in Europe. He went on to produce the first complete account of the human body and how to dissect it, and his drawings set the standard for anatomical art for centuries to come, earning him the title of 'the founder of modern anatomy'(n)

programme.title: London: A Tale of Two Cities with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian examines how London coped with a series of disastrous events in the 17th century, including the Great Fire, the Plague and the Civil War, and became one of Europe's most powerful cities. He uses information from surveys taken by chroniclers at the beginning and end of the century to explore the qualities that enabled Londoners to thrive, and explains how events of the period laid the foundations for the English capital's success(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Tomb on Earth: Secrets of Ancient China
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Alice Roberts and Albert Lin explore the final resting place of China's first emperor, a site 200 times larger than Egypt's Valley of the Kings and home to the famous Terracotta Warriors. Making use of both the latest forensic technology and ancient texts, they delve into this historic site's secrets and uncover evidence that casts a new light on the founding of the nation(n)

programme.title: Contagion: The BBC Four Pandemic
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry and emergency medic Dr Javid Abdelmoneim front this experiment to test the threat of a new flu pandemic to the UK. An app is used to 'infect' users and track their movements and social interactions over 24 hours, with Hannah adopting the role of Patient Zero as she walks the streets of Haslemere in Surrey to launch the outbreak, while Javid investigates why flu is such a danger to society and visits a factory that will produce vaccine when the next virus emerges(n)

programme.title: Reporting History: Mandela and a New South Africa
programme.desc: On the 25th anniversary of Mandela's election as South African president, Fergal Keane goes back to examine his reports and considers why history did not turn out how he expected(n)

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's to chart a year in the life of the international institution, recording the highs and lows of life in the art business, from London to Dubai, via Hong Kong and New York(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Big Fat Gypsy Wedding dressmaker Thelma Madine takes on the world of the feis with a unique range of outfits for dancers(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: After stepping out in style on Britain's Got Talent in 2014, the Innova dance troupe are brought down to earth when they head back to the beaches of Portstewart(n)

programme.title: London: A Tale of Two Cities with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian examines how London coped with a series of disastrous events in the 17th century, including the Great Fire, the Plague and the Civil War, and became one of Europe's most powerful cities. He uses information from surveys taken by chroniclers at the beginning and end of the century to explore the qualities that enabled Londoners to thrive, and explains how events of the period laid the foundations for the English capital's success(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford travels to the Hague and Amsterdam to find out what inspired artists such as Rembrandt and Ruysch to create anatomical paintings in the 17th century, and learns how dissecting bodies became considered a suitable subject for art in the period(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Robert Llewellyn visits Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus's repair facility, where carriages are dispatched for annual MOTs, while Anita Rani samples a commute by road in Mumbai(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Musician Angela Moran looks at the history of Birmingham's Irish residents, talking to local people and presenting rare archive footage. She discovers the impact that the 1974 pub bombings had on the community, many of whom felt the need to disguise their heritage, and recalls her own experiences of growing up in the 1990s, when being Irish became fashionable(n)

programme.title: Ireland's Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the Irish artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the age of the Romantics, and the flamboyant George IV who created so much of the visual look of the modern monarchy. He also looks back the lives of at Queen Victoria and Prince Albert, for whom collecting was an integral part of their happy marriage(n)

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: The second of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's, where Global President Jussi Pylkkanen and his team continue with their big push into Asia. As the company prepares for its sale to celebrate its 250th anniversary, an umbrella stand turns out to be worth millions and a Rubens appears that has not been seen in public for 150 years(n)

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry is joined by actor Paul McGann and psychotherapist Philippa Perry to explore the technology, art and culture of the last years of Queen Victoria's reign. In the first episode, Hannah investigates how innovations in science and technology - including the X-ray, safety bicycle and proto-aeroplane - transformed Britain in the 1890s, with the end of the 19th century marking the beginning of a new 'Electric Age'(n)

programme.title: Pedalling Dreams: The Raleigh Story
programme.desc: Documentary telling the tale of the ups and downs of Raleigh bikes, using personal testimony and rare and previously unseen archive film. Born in the back streets of Nottingham in 1888, Raleigh grew to become the biggest bicycle manufacturer in the world and, for more than a century, was known for its simple and practical bikes(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Robert Llewellyn visits Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus's repair facility, where carriages are dispatched for annual MOTs, while Anita Rani samples a commute by road in Mumbai(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Polar adventurer Paul Rose heads for the North Pennines in the latest stage of his journey. He goes white-water rafting down the River Tees - and takes in one of Britain's best views at High Cup Nick. He also hears about a weather phenomenon unique to the Pennine Way and spends a night at a remote mountain refuge close to the highest point of the route(n)

programme.title: Walt Disney
programme.desc: The first of two documentaries exploring the life, career and legacy of Walt Disney, making use of archive footage alongside interviews with his friends, colleagues and relatives to present a portrait of a ruthlessly driven animator and entrepreneur whose single-minded ambition allowed him to create a string of cartoon masterpieces that would define an entire industry. This opening instalment charts Disney's early days, from the creation of Mickey Mouse through to the triumph of Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, his first full-length animated feature film(n)

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The British director reveals the films that have influenced her life and career, including One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, The Talented Mr Ripley, Brokeback Mountain and Claire Denis's French masterpiece Beau Travail. Sam also provides insight into the making of her most recent movie, A Million Little Pieces, and reflects on her early introduction to cinema, as well as her experience of moving to Hollywood to live and work(n)

programme.title: Inside Cinema: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at family in cinema in all their maddening, dysfunctional glory, with clans in Aliens, Back to the Future, Home Alone, Tokyo Story, Pan's Labyrinth and Gosford Park among those analysed. Kathy Burke narrates(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda berates Mick for talking to the social worker about her and sets out to put the woman straight, while Tina questions whether Ollie is safe around his mother, leaving Mick conflicted. Whitney opens up to Kush about a letter she kept from Tony, leaving Leo intrigued as he listens in. Sonia is distraught to learn that Bex is moving out, Sharon and Dennis finally reach an understanding and Jean is upset when Daniel snaps at her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron plans to give Chloe his mother's necklace as a Valentine's Day gift - but first he needs to get it back from Nicky. She has problems of her own - she's pawned Bernie's necklace to pay her mother's debts, but is still being harassed for more money, forcing her to put her life and career on the line. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Cadi and Vaughan are called to investigate when a body is discovered in a house outside Blaenau Ffestiniog. Meanwhile three youngsters, Mia, Connor and Lee, try to return to a normal life they have lost. Return of the crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams and Siôn Alun Davies(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Return of the legal drama, starring Nicola Walker. A death in the family brings everyone back together. While Hannah's marriage seems to be back on track, she has begun a dangerous affair with Christie. Zander returns from America, and introduces an unsettling new addition to the team. Nathan takes on a protegee, and the firm takes on two high-profile clients - a TV presenter locked in an unhappy and controlling marriage and a man who wants a divorce before he dies. Guest starring Donna Air(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Four women watch live breast reduction as they consider going under the knife. They share their reasons for thinking about surgery, many of which are often more practical than aesthetic, including back pain and limitations on what they can do with their children(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern help members of the Humberside Police force combat their sedentary behaviour as they challenge them to bring more movement into their daily routines, fitting them with movement trackers to check their progress. Meanwhile, Hugh tests one of the most popular health crazes to hit the market, CBD, on three volunteers who suffer from insomnia, and Steph meets a family struggling with issues surrounding their body image(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A desert island survival course goes wrong when the leader - a trained survival expert and ex-SAS soldier - is found dead in the forest of a remote island near Saint Marie. Having ventured off into the wilderness alone and with no other people on the island apart from the trip participants, suspicion soon falls upon them. But with all of the course members providing one another with an alibi, how could any of them have committed the murder? That's what new detective inspector Neville intends to find out. Starring Ralf Little(n)

programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a millionaire who let the public fund his car racing hobby, and the boss of a recycling firm who helped himself to millions in government money(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore a former mill in Preston, a canalside cottage in Stourport-on-Severn and a terraced house in Chiswick(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mum Rachael thinks she and her son Harry should move to Australia for a better quality of life. Harry, however, is not so sure, so the pair spend a trial week in Brisbane(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Fly-tippers are caught on camera loading their truck with 150 bags of waste before dumping it in the Rhondda Valley, and a training centre for illegal dog fighting is raided(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Tim Weeks and Colin Young help two teams find items at an antique centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about the history of local landscape with artist David Walker Barker(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A grieving mother takes the law into her own hands to get justice for her son, and Karen thwarts Al and Bear's plans to make the Mill's system automatic(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps two teachers find the ideal country home in Lancashire for their four children, and also pays a visit to one of the last working forges in the country(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Pig farmer Keith and budding herb grower Peter battle the temperamental British weather to get their premium produce to families and friends at Blackheath Farmers' Market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Tim Medhurst continue in the south west of England, where Christina goes caving to uncover the little-known story of maturing cheddar cheese(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda berates Mick for talking to the social worker about her and sets out to put the woman straight, while Tina questions whether Ollie is safe around his mother, leaving Mick conflicted. Whitney opens up to Kush about a letter she kept from Tony, leaving Leo intrigued as he listens in. Sonia is distraught to learn that Bex is moving out, Sharon and Dennis finally reach an understanding and Jean is upset when Daniel snaps at her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron plans to give Chloe his mother's necklace as a Valentine's Day gift - but first he needs to get it back from Nicky. She has problems of her own - she's pawned Bernie's necklace to pay her mother's debts, but is still being harassed for more money, forcing her to put her life and career on the line. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Return of the legal drama, starring Nicola Walker. A death in the family brings everyone back together. While Hannah's marriage seems to be back on track, she has begun a dangerous affair with Christie. Zander returns from America, and introduces an unsettling new addition to the team. Nathan takes on a protegee, and the firm takes on two high-profile clients - a TV presenter locked in an unhappy and controlling marriage and a man who wants a divorce before he dies. Guest starring Donna Air(n)

programme.title: The Search
programme.desc: Volunteers are called to a hospital where a patient has disappeared sparking a major search. Meanwhile in Belfast, teams search for a man with mental health issues(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Four women watch live breast reduction as they consider going under the knife. They share their reasons for thinking about surgery, many of which are often more practical than aesthetic, including back pain and limitations on what they can do with their children(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: A serial insurance fraudster tries to cash in on horrific terror attacks, and the former taxman who devised a scam to con the public out of nearly £7 million(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows the Edwards family during a trial week in Perth as mum Shana tries to persuade reluctant husband Philip and their two older children, who are adamant about not going, that Australia is the life for them(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers catch up with a travel company boss who has been conning holiday-makers, and crack down on a water company after a sewage leak pollutes a stream in Oxfordshire(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper help the red and blue teams search for valuable items at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthen, in the hope of making a profit at auction. Plus, host Eric Knowles travels to a small seaside town in south Wales to find out how a stained glass window, made by local artist John Petts, became an iconic image of the American Civil Rights movement in the 1960s(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A newly engaged couple are joined by Jules Hudson on a house-hunting mission in south Worcestershire, looking to purchase their first joint home with a budget of £300,000(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Robin goes head to head with 81-year-old breeder Di in the National Competition at the Royal Norfolk Show, showcasing their diminutive Dexter cattle(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern help members of the Humberside Police force combat their sedentary behaviour as they challenge them to bring more movement into their daily routines, fitting them with movement trackers to check their progress. Meanwhile, Hugh tests one of the most popular health crazes to hit the market, CBD, on three volunteers who suffer from insomnia, and Steph meets a family struggling with issues surrounding their body image(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Goffy wants Danielle to know he is sorry for leading a double life. Meanwhile, Aoife has returned from Australia and Conner is keen to start things up again(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Cardiff-based 20-year-old hopeless romantic Lewis is scaring away potential partners by talking wedding plans on the first date(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

A night out for the Albert Square youngsters start badly as Dennis is less than pleased to see Bobby - and things quickly go downhill from there(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A desert island survival course goes wrong when the leader - a trained survival expert and ex-SAS soldier - is found dead in the forest of a remote island near Saint Marie. Having ventured off into the wilderness alone and with no other people on the island apart from the trip participants, suspicion soon falls upon them. But with all of the course members providing one another with an alibi, how could any of them have committed the murder? That's what new detective inspector Neville intends to find out. Starring Ralf Little(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Callie Lewis was just 24 when she took her own life. She had been in the care of the NHS, but fell through the cracks of an understaffed and overstretched system. For the last 16 months, Panorama has been filming with her family as they tried to understand what went wrong. Shortly before she died, they learned that Callie was a member of an online suicide forum, where she found detailed advice on how to kill herself. As reporter Ellie Flynn discovers, Callie's death comes at a time when many people with mental health issues continue to complain about their struggle to connect with the services they need(n)

programme.desc: An extraordinary story that has affected two islands, which reveals the close relationship that has developed between two families. A few years ago Isle of Scalpay resident Kenny Macleod received news from another country that someone he did not know was looking for him as they were sure they shared the same father(n)

programme.title: Oran na Mna
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis looks at how the themes of love and loss are portrayed in the Gaelic poetry and songs of women(n)

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Neil Campbell and Ewen Henderson perform Psalm 6 and Cumbernauld Gaelic Choir sing Subhachas nam Chridh'. Alasdair Morrison shares the role faith plays in his life, and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs(n)

programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon visits Glenfinnan and Ardnamurchan, home to steam trains, spectacular scenery, Jacobites, music, and a very famous wizard(n)

programme.title: Fo Ghlas (World's Toughest Prisons)
programme.desc: Antanimora Prison, Madagascar, which though built for 800 prisoners houses more than 3,000. They sleep crammed together, body against body. The cells are in darkness, overrun with cockroaches, lice and the unbearable odour of latrines(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants who have never met before pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon gets an unexpected visitor, Matty refuses Cain's help, and Belle makes a decision(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: All hands are on deck as the metro system prepares for the busiest day of the year - the Great North Run. This year, a new and untested station in South Shields is at the centre of things. One of the event's regular runners prepares for a personal return to the course, as he runs in tribute to a friend(n)

programme.title: The BRITs at 40
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall narrates a look back at the history of the awards ceremony, featuring archive footage of some of the most memorable moments, from Oasis and Blur's rivalry to Stormzy's attack on the government, and classic performances by Adele, Rihanna and Drake. The programme also recalls less successful moments, from the infamous 1989 ceremony hosted by Mick Fleetwood and Sam Fox, and Madonna struggles with her cape. Featuring interviews with stars including Robbie Williams, Mel B, Mick Fleetwood, Little Mix, Sam Smith, Ellie Goulding, Sharon Osbourne, Bros, Dua Lipa, Rick Astley and Tom Jones(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: With the testimony of a vital witness confirming some of Stan and Mick's suspicions, Taff still remains unconvinced, but the police are now forced to question what they thought they knew previously, and pursue the case against Jeremy. Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. Starring Mark Addy, Alexa Davies, Freddie Fox and Tom Christian(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their journey, the pair continue through the snow to New Hampshire. Here, they meet rally-driving champion Tim O'Neil who gives them a crash course in driving in the ice and snow before they take on an intense time trial. The pair travel on to Maine, where they take part in some wilderness training, before bidding farewell to the RV and heading out into the extremely choppy seas for some lobster fishing. Before heading home, the pair look back on some of their favourite moments from the trip(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi is shocked to hear the allegations about Ray, while Maria hands David her keys, telling him she would like him to have her car. Ryan hands Gary an envelope full of cash and confirms it's the last of his debt collections(n)

programme.desc: In 10 years Airbnb has grown into a multimillion-dollar global entity - and revolutionised the way people travel. But there are claims that short-term lets not only threaten the future of traditional hotels, but could be contributing to housing shortages - as well as driving up the cost of properties. Jonathan Maitland investigates(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: The British love chocolate, so Sian Williams investigates what makes it so irresistible and gets the public tucking into the Cadbury's chocolate bar with 30 per cent less sugar to see if it as tasty as it is healthy. Two more diets are also put to the test - the Meal Prepping for Weight-loss Diet, in which calorie-counted meals are all prepared in advance, and the Diabetes Weight-loss cookbook, road-tested by a woman who is borderline type 2 diabetic(n)

programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The opening instalment in a three-part documentary following the boxer as he prepares for his rematch with Deontay Wilder for the WBC World Heavyweight title, after the first contest in December 2018 ended in a much-disputed draw. Filmed over several months, the programme provides an insight into the life of one of British sport's most flamboyant and controversial characters and those who are closest to him(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Keen to unwind, Geordie and Will head to a charity boxing match at Vic's gym. However, as Matthew and Luke face off in the ring the bout takes an unexpected twist. The pair travel on to Maine, where they take part in some wilderness training, before bidding farewell to the RV and heading out into the extremely choppy seas for some lobster fishing. Before heading home, the pair look back on some of their favourite moments from the trip(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A blood-spattered hotel room and a stolen credit card lead Ronnie and Joe to Charles Hutton, a member of the wealthy upper classes who dotes on his wife Camille and daughter Georgia. The detectives find clues that suggest Georgia gave birth at the scene, but where is the baby now? The young woman and her boyfriend Rufus prove difficult for Jake and Kate to prosecute, and to make matters worse, the defence counsel turns out to be Jake's old nemesis. As the evidence stacks up, how far will Charles go to protect his privileged family? Guest starring James Wilby and Joseph Millson(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's online attack on Andrew yields unexpected consequences as a figure from her past returns to haunt her, potentially jeopardising the case. Laura resorts to extreme measures to find hard evidence of Andrew's guilt(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a series of shootings claims one of their own, Ronnie and Joe have little time to grieve - they must solve the case before the killer reaches their next target on a hit list that includes CPS director Henry Sharpe. It gradually becomes apparent that the common thread connecting all the names is the case of Mark Glendon, convicted of the murder of his wife four years earlier. Although he's still in custody, a retrial is imminent, putting pressure on the team to catch the murderer before the jury delivers its verdict(n)

programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Frenchwoman arrives to help with preparations for Aidensfield's twinning with her home town, but things go wrong when she is left to the tender mercies of Peggy. Rob marks his return to the village by saving a con-man after his car crashes into the river, while district nurse Carol Cassidy struggles to treat a woman with an over-protective husband. Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay star(n)

programme.desc: Mavis is furious when Angela's secretary summons her, and Derek tells Mavis he is returning home. Sally is fed up with being pregnant and feels she will never go into labour(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Frenchwoman arrives to help with preparations for Aidensfield's twinning with her home town, but things go wrong when she is left to the tender mercies of Peggy. Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Still reeling from the death of one of their own, Ronnie and Joe face a new challenge when an innocent young mum is fatally stabbed in a busy marketplace, just as a senior police officer publicly pledges to crack down on knife crime. Under pressure from all sides, the pair struggle to gather evidence against the youth they think is responsible, and the suspect's unintentional confession appears to bring the case to a close. However, Ronnie is the only person who hears the admission, and it proves difficult getting anyone to believe him. With the detective's honesty cast into doubt and his effectiveness questioned at every turn, is this the end of his career? With Tony Gardner, Sharon Small and Colin Salmon(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura travels to Edinburgh to try to uncover the truth behind Andrew's wife's death, but finds more than she bargained for. Meanwhile, tensions between Tom and Katy reach breaking point as their secret threatens to surface. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek is upset when Roger gives Mavis the portrait to keep, and also receives a letter from Angela's husband. Bet tells Charlie she can never trust him again(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis accuses Derek of being unfaithful, and Curly is irritated when everyone asks him why he was sacked. Gail is furious when she finds Nick working when he is too ill for school(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Kim about the suspicious noises coming from Mill Cottage. Ned plans to go ahead with the fight despite family protests. Alan unveils his home brew at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott is grounded for stealing fireworks, Caroline invites Eric to the Tates' bonfire, Jack tells Ned he is scared he might lose Sarah, and Luke apologises to Ned for the fight(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield's policemen struggle to deduce who could be behind a spate of sheep rustling, while Peggy is intrigued by an eco-warrior couple's slurry-fuelled generator. Helen is called in after the body of an elderly woman is found in the river, while the dead woman's son finds it difficult to deal with the consequences of his mother's demise. Drama, starring Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Taxman Charley decides to escape from the office and go strawberry-picking with Mariette and the rest of the Larkin family - a move that leaves his career hanging in the balance. Light-hearted drama based on the novels by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a woman is involved in a hit-and-run following a local dance, Miller finds himself racing to discover the identity of the culprit. Meanwhile, David decides he has had enough of Peggy's miserly ways and walks out on the taxi service(n)

programme.desc: Eric makes his preparations for the robbery, Kim offers Biff a job at Heritage Farm, and Kathy witnesses Rachel and Chris kissing at the fireworks display(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The morning after the robbery, Eric wakes up Alan telling him how drunk they were the night before. Sarah tells Jack she does not want to leave the farm(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet urges Raquel to put things straight with Curly, Emily is embarrassed when Samir gives her money to pay back Deirdre's loan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a woman is involved in a hit-and-run following a local dance, Miller finds himself racing to discover the identity of the culprit. Meanwhile, David decides he has had enough of Peggy's miserly ways and walks out on the taxi service(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A blood-spattered hotel room and a stolen credit card lead Ronnie and Joe to Charles Hutton, a member of the wealthy upper classes who dotes on his wife Camille and daughter Georgia. The detectives find clues that suggest Georgia gave birth at the scene, but where is the baby now? The young woman and her boyfriend Rufus prove difficult for Jake and Kate to prosecute, and to make matters worse, the defence counsel turns out to be Jake's old nemesis. As the evidence stacks up, how far will Charles go to protect his privileged family? Guest starring James Wilby and Joseph Millson(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's online attack on Andrew yields unexpected consequences as a figure from her past returns to haunt her, potentially jeopardising the case. Laura resorts to extreme measures to find hard evidence of Andrew's guilt(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)(n)
